note: Per-run records. worldgen = macro-averaged per-block-class accuracy vs the real Java game over time-seeds drawn after the run froze (v0 scores the worldgen leg; sim tick-trace and fidelity-gated SPS land next). Raw artifacts: benchmarks/rewrite/results/runs/. Prompt is versioned (SPEC section 3); scores compare within a prompt version only. All v1 runs used a fixed 8h budget with no stated bar; v2 states the bit-exact bar with no stated time limit.

runs.note: told 'do not stop until bit-for-bit' with no time limit: ran 21 min (vs 9 under v1), best water (91%) and lava (58%) of any run, but lost its v1 dirt/grass gains; ended with a clean EndTurn whose final message is a TODO list of its own remaining bugs

told 'do not stop until bit-for-bit' with no time limit: ran 19 min (vs 15 under v1), score unchanged from v1 (18.3 to 18.57), and its sign-off explicitly concedes 'this is not bit-for-bit Minecraft 1.11.2'

self-terminated at 9 min of 8h and still tops the board: first to place dirt/grass (70%) and sand/gravel (30%), first nonzero ores/leaves/wood; signed off with 'structurally correct, not bit-identical to Java yet'

INFRA, not a fail: killed mid-refactor by Max-plan credit exhaustion (429 wall at 61 min, 112 turns, 1.24M output tokens), leaving the tree uncompilable. Also the only run attempting vanilla-exact worldgen (Java Random, noise, carving) and the only one using git. Rerun pending
first to crack sea level - water 88%, lava 55%, air 99.5% - and raw match ties all-stone while macro is +11 over it; ores/trees/caves still ~0%
grinds longest of the sweep and first to place real water (50%) - but ores, trees, vegetation, caves still ~0%
best stone shaping of the sweep (78.8%) but nothing beyond air/stone/bedrock; initial eval-container header mismatch was a harness bug, rescored after parity fix
builds, but violates the dump contract twice: 32-byte .mcbd header (drops dim/reserved fields) and --out path handling writes to a corrupted filename - no scoreable dump
cheapest scored run; air/stone/bedrock only, weakest air placement of the sweep (83.5%) - everything else ~0%
self-terminated at 15 min of 8h; ABI/CLI/CUDA stepping built clean in the air-gapped container, but worldgen is an admitted approximation - ores, trees, water, caves near 0%

baselines.name: all-stone
baselines.note: trivial floor: bedrock + stone to y63 + air; beats gpt-5.5 on raw match
